whyItLookedAI: A troll version with six fingers went viral, making people doubt the real photo too.
story: Evan Vucci captured Trump raising his fist with blood on his face seconds after being shot at a Butler, Pennsylvania rally on July 13, 2024. A doctored version with a sixth finger circulated widely. People started questioning whether the original AP photo was also AI-generated.

whyItLookedAI: Telephoto compression flattened 15,000 people into a uniform crowd that looked artificially generated.
story: Andrew Harnik shot this overhead view of roughly 15,000 supporters on the tarmac at Detroit Metropolitan Airport during a Kamala Harris rally in August 2024. The telephoto lens compressed the depth. The massive crowd looks flat and unnaturally uniform.

whyItLookedAI: The sheer scale of the crowd and dramatic composition looked too perfect to be a spontaneous photograph.
story: Arash Khamooshi photographed a massive crowd filling Revolution Square in Tehran for the New York Times in March 2026. The scale and composition seemed impossibly dramatic for a news photo.

whyItLookedAI: Telephoto compression made the Coronado Bridge and skyline appear impossibly close to the breaking waves.
story: Randy Dible shot waves breaking with the Coronado Bridge and San Diego skyline compressed behind them. PetaPixel reported he had two separate sets of photos falsely accused of being AI on the same day. The extreme telephoto compression creates a perspective that looks physically impossible.

whyItLookedAI: Instagram automatically labeled this real photo 'Made with AI' because the photographer used Adobe tools that triggered the AI metadata flag.
story: Peter Yan's Mount Fuji landscape was auto-labeled 'Made with AI' by Instagram because Adobe Creative Cloud tools added AI metadata tags even when no generative AI was used. PetaPixel covered the story as an example of overzealous AI detection harming real photographers.

whyItLookedAI: Glowing geometric light forms hovering above a landscape look exactly like something typed into a prompt.
story: Reuben Wu uses GPS-programmed drones carrying LED lights in long exposures to create his Siren series. The glowing curtain-like forms above Lake Michigan are real light captured in-camera. LensCulture published the series with the subtitle 'No, These Photos Are Not AI.'

explanation: A Brazilian fan created this with AI. Katy Perry never attended the 2024 Met Gala. Her own mother was fooled.

annotations.label: Melted faces
annotations.description: Photographer faces in the background blur and merge together.

annotations.label: Warped hands
annotations.description: Background photographers have misshapen, distorted hands on their cameras.

annotations.label: Missing arm
annotations.description: One of Perry's arms is absent from the image.

annotations.label: Skin-fabric blend
annotations.description: Dress fabric merges into her skin instead of sitting on top of the body.

annotations.label: Wrong carpet
annotations.description: The staircase carpet matches the 2018 Met Gala, not the 2024 event.

explanation: The most-shared image on Instagram ever with 50M+ shares. The tent rows are unnaturally uniform and the snow-capped mountains look nothing like real Rafah.

annotations.label: Uniform tent rows
annotations.description: Tents are arranged in perfect, repeating grid patterns. Real refugee camps are chaotic.

annotations.label: Fictional mountains
annotations.description: Snow-capped mountains and golden light look nothing like the flat terrain of real Rafah.

annotations.label: No people
annotations.description: The entire camp is empty. No human figures, vehicles, belongings, or debris anywhere.

annotations.label: Inconsistent shadows
annotations.description: Shadow directions contradict each other across different parts of the scene.

explanation: Circulated during Hurricane Helene as a real rescue photo. Multiple versions exist with different dog colors and boat colors. That alone proves it was generated.

annotations.label: Missing finger
annotations.description: The girl's left hand has only four fingers.

annotations.label: Glossy skin
annotations.description: Unnaturally smooth, porcelain-like skin with no pores or blemishes.

annotations.label: Warped boat floor
annotations.description: Wooden slats do not follow correct perspective geometry.

annotations.label: Vanishing boat
annotations.description: A boat in the background fades and disappears unnaturally.

annotations.label: Life jacket reflector
annotations.description: The reflector strip changes between different versions of this image.

explanation: Google Gemini generated racially diverse Nazi soldiers when asked for WWII images. Google pulled the feature within days. The historical impossibility was the obvious tell.

annotations.label: Garbled insignia
annotations.description: Helmet symbols are unreadable glyphs instead of actual WWII-era markings.

annotations.label: Garbled insignia
annotations.description: Right helmet has distorted symbols that do not match any real military unit.

annotations.label: Waxy skin
annotations.description: Skin is unnaturally smooth and pore-less with an airbrushed quality.

annotations.label: Wrong uniform details
annotations.description: Collar tabs and patches do not correspond to any real military branch or rank.

annotations.label: Anachronistic patches
annotations.description: Shoulder insignia are generic composites, not accurate reproductions.

explanation: AI spam farms created 'Shrimp Jesus' images as Facebook engagement bait. The impossible composition and merging of human and crustacean forms are obvious AI artifacts.

annotations.label: Left hand
annotations.description: Fingers merge with shrimp legs. No clear boundary between hand and crustacean.

annotations.label: Right hand
annotations.description: Same merging on the other side. Fingers and shrimp appendages fuse together.

annotations.label: Waxy face
annotations.description: Skin is too smooth and glassy. The expression is flat and affect-less.

annotations.label: Body-shell fusion
annotations.description: Human torso and shrimp shell blend together with no coherent boundary.

annotations.label: Halo confusion
annotations.description: Crown of thorns and halo are mashed together. No real artist would mix these.

explanation: This AI-generated baby first appeared during the Turkey-Syria earthquake in 2023, then was recycled during the Gaza conflict. Extra fingers and unnatural forehead creases give it away.

annotations.label: Extra fingers
annotations.description: The baby's hand has more than five fingers with deformed, curling shapes.

annotations.label: Forehead creases
annotations.description: Wrinkle pattern on the forehead does not match how real infant skin creases.

annotations.label: Exaggerated chin
annotations.description: The chin area is distorted beyond what natural anatomy produces.

annotations.label: Unnatural eye shimmer
annotations.description: Eyes shimmer with light that does not match the scene's actual lighting.
